title: coreopsis peruviana
intro: Coreopsis peruviana, also known as the Peruvian tickseed or Goldthread, is a flowering plant native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the aster family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Coreopsis peruviana include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support overall well-being and prevent illness.
